title: OAuth, Secrets, Identity, and Least Privilege · Integration Triage
summary: Secure a remote MCP server serving user-specific data. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an OAuth and credential-handling design and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Decide when authentication and user authorization are required by data and action scope.
Implement protected resource metadata and OAuth-aligned authorization for remote HTTP.
Validate token audience, issuer, scopes, expiry, and resource binding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Least Privilege Tools applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map scopes and approvals to narrow tools and user intent.
